topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The glow of the laptop screen was an unwelcome intrusion into my evening. I’d been cleaning out Leo’s old tablet, a task I’d been dreading, and then I stumbled across it. A hidden folder, labeled cryptically, containing a completely separate social media profile. My jaw tightened. I felt a slow, burning resentment rise in my chest, a hot, acidic feeling that made my hands clench into fists on the desk. I leaned closer, the reflection of the screen dancing in my eyes. It was a world he kept walled off, a curated existence I hadn't been privy to. I didn't want to know. I just wanted to erase the evidence, to make this whole discovery disappear, to regain some sense of control over the chaotic mess he’d become.

[story 2]

"Seriously, another secret?" I muttered, the words tasting like ash in my mouth. I watched Leo across the room, his headphones firmly in place, oblivious to the silent war waging inside me. The discovery—a secret online life—ignited a sharp, bitter feeling. It wasn't just curiosity; it was a profound sense of being shut out, of being deliberately kept in the dark. I stood up, the floorboards creaking under my weight, and walked over to his room. My footsteps were heavy, deliberate. When I finally sat on the edge of his bed, I didn't speak immediately. I just stared at the device, a cold, hard resolve settling over me. I needed to dismantle this illusion, to expose the carefully constructed façade he’d built away from me.

[story 3]

I picked up the phone, the screen illuminating my face with a harsh, unforgiving light. The details of the hidden account were laid bare—photos, cryptic posts, a whole persona I never knew existed. A tight knot formed beneath my ribs, a feeling of being utterly undermined. I felt a surge of pure irritation, the kind that makes your skin crawl. Why the deception? Why hide this other self? I scrolled through the feed, a cold, calculating energy taking over. Every carefully chosen caption felt like a deliberate slight against the reality I thought I shared with my son. I didn't feel sadness; I felt a fierce, protective anger, the kind that demands immediate correction.
